```{r}
  kw_results_inundation$df[i] <- df_val
  kw_results_inundation$p_value[i] <- p_val
  kw_results_inundation$Significant[i] <- ifelse(p_val < 0.05, "YES", "NO")
  
  # Display results
  cat("Kruskal-Wallis Test:\n")
  cat("  χ² =", round(chi_sq, 3), "\n")
  cat("  df =", df_val, "\n")
  cat("  p-value =", format.pval(p_val, digits = 3), "\n")
  
  if(p_val < 0.05) {
    cat("  ✓ SIGNIFICANT (p < 0.05)\n")
    cat("  → At least one landscape-inundation combination differs\n")
  } else {
    cat("  ✗ NOT SIGNIFICANT (p >= 0.05)\n")
    cat("  → No evidence of differences among combinations\n")
  }
  
  # -------------------------------------------------------------------
  # If significant, run Dunn's Post-hoc Test
  # -------------------------------------------------------------------
  if(p_val < 0.05) {
    cat("\n  ═══════════════════════════════════════\n")
    cat("  DUNN'S POST-HOC TEST FOR:", var_label, "\n")
    cat("  ═══════════════════════════════════════\n")
    cat("  (This may take a moment - many pairwise comparisons)\n")
    
    dunn_test <- dunnTest(as.formula(formula_str), 
                          data = df_filtered, 
                          method = "bh")
    
    # Store results
    dunn_results_inundation[[var_short[i]]] <- dunn_test$res
    
    # Display only significant pairwise comparisons
    sig_pairs <- dunn_test$res %>% 
      filter(P.adj < 0.05) %>%
      arrange(P.adj) %>%
      mutate(
        Z = round(Z, 3),
        P.adj = round(P.adj, 4)
      )
    
    if(nrow(sig_pairs) > 0) {
      cat("\n  ✓ Significant pairwise differences (p < 0.05):\n\n")
      print(sig_pairs %>% select(Comparison, Z, P.adj))
      
      cat("\n  Total significant pairs:", nrow(sig_pairs), 
          "out of", nrow(dunn_test$res), "\n")
    } else {
      cat("\n  Note: Overall test significant but no pairwise differences\n")
      cat("        after correction for multiple comparisons\n")
    }
    
    cat("  ═══════════════════════════════════════\n")
    
    # Save full Dunn's test results for this variable (commented out)
    # write.csv(dunn_test$res, 
    #           paste0("dunn_test_", var, "_inundation.csv"), 
    #           row.names = FALSE)
    
  } else {
    cat("\n  (Skipping post-hoc test - overall test not significant)\n")
    dunn_results_inundation[[var_short[i]]] <- NULL
  }
}

# Display summary table
cat("\n\n==========================================\n")
cat("SUMMARY: LANDSCAPE × INUNDATION ANALYSIS\n")
cat("==========================================\n\n")
print(kw_results_inundation)

# Save results (commented out)
# write.csv(kw_results_inundation, 
#           "kruskal_wallis_inundation_results.csv", 
#           row.names = FALSE)

# ===================================================================
# PART E: CALCULATE DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS
# ===================================================================

cat("\n\n==========================================\n")
cat("DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS\n")
cat("==========================================\n")

# Calculate means and SDs by landscape position
cat("\nBy Landscape Position:\n")
summary_landscape <- df_wide %>%
  group_by(landscape_position) %>%
  summarise(
    across(all_of(env_vars), 
           list(mean = ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE),
                sd = ~sd(., na.rm = TRUE)),
           .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"),
    n = n()
  )

print(summary_landscape)

# Save summary statistics (commented out)
# write.csv(summary_landscape, 
#           "summary_stats_landscape.csv", 
#           row.names = FALSE)

# Calculate means and SDs by landscape × inundation
cat("\n\nBy Landscape Position × Inundation:\n")
summary_inundation <- df_filtered %>%
  group_by(landscape_position, inundated) %>%
  summarise(
    across(all_of(env_vars), 
           list(mean = ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE),
                sd = ~sd(., na.rm = TRUE)),
           .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"),
    n = n(),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(landscape_position, inundated)

print(summary_inundation)

# Save summary statistics (commented out)
# write.csv(summary_inundation, 
#           "summary_stats_inundation.csv", 
#           row.names = FALSE)

# ===================================================================
# PART F: CREATE LETTER GROUPINGS FOR PLOTS
# ===================================================================

cat("\n\n==========================================\n")
cat("CREATING LETTER GROUPINGS FOR PLOTS\n")
cat("==========================================\n")

# This function assigns letters (a, b, c, etc.) based on Dunn's test
# Groups that share a letter are NOT significantly different
assign_letters_from_dunn <- function(dunn_results, groups) {
  
  # If no Dunn results (test not significant), all groups get same letter
  if(is.null(dunn_results)) {
    letters_df <- data.frame(
      group = groups,
      letters = rep("a", length(groups))
    )
    return(letters_df)
  }
  
  # Initialize all groups with empty letters
  letter_assignment <- data.frame(
    group = groups,
    letters = "",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Get significant pairs (p < 0.05)
  sig_pairs <- dunn_results %>% filter(P.adj < 0.05)
  
  # Simple algorithm: assign letters based on pairwise differences
  # Groups that are NOT significantly different share letters
  
  current_letter <- 1  # Start with 'a'
  
  for(i in 1:length(groups)) {
    current_group <- groups[i]
    
    if(letter_assignment$letters[i] == "") {
      # Assign next available letter
      letter_assignment$letters[i] <- letters[current_letter]
      
      # Find all groups NOT significantly different from current group
      for(j in (i+1):length(groups)) {
        if(j > length(groups)) break
        
        compare_group <- groups[j]
        
        # Check if these two groups are significantly different
        comparison1 <- paste(current_group, compare_group, sep = " - ")
        comparison2 <- paste(compare_group, current_group, sep = " - ")
        
        is_different <- any(sig_pairs$Comparison %in% c(comparison1, comparison2))
        
        # If NOT different, assign same letter
        if(!is_different && letter_assignment$letters[j] == "") {
          letter_assignment$letters[j] <- letters[current_letter]
        }
      }
      
      current_letter <- current_letter + 1
    }
  }
  
  return(letter_assignment)
}

# Create letter assignments for landscape position
cat("\nLetter assignments for LANDSCAPE POSITION:\n")
cat("(Groups with same letter are not significantly different)\n\n")

landscape_letters_list <- list()

for(i in 1:length(env_vars)) {
  var_label <- var_short[i]
  
  cat(var_label, ":\n")
  
  groups <- c("lowland", "slope", "upland")
  letters_df <- assign_letters_from_dunn(dunn_results_landscape[[var_label]], 
                                         groups)
  
  colnames(letters_df) <- c("landscape_position", "letters")
  landscape_letters_list[[var_label]] <- letters_df
  
  print(letters_df)
  cat("\n")
}

# Create letter assignments for landscape × inundation
cat("\nLetter assignments for LANDSCAPE × INUNDATION:\n")
cat("(Groups with same letter are not significantly different)\n\n")

inundation_letters_list <- list()

for(i in 1:length(env_vars)) {
  var_label <- var_short[i]
  
  cat(var_label, ":\n")
  
  groups <- unique(df_filtered$landscape_inundation)
  letters_df <- assign_letters_from_dunn(dunn_results_inundation[[var_label]], 
                                         groups)
  
  colnames(letters_df) <- c("landscape_inundation", "letters")
  inundation_letters_list[[var_label]] <- letters_df
  
  print(letters_df)
  cat("\n")
}

# ===================================================================
# PART G: SAVE LETTER ASSIGNMENTS FOR PLOTTING
# ===================================================================

# These letter assignments can be merged with your plotting data
# to add significance labels to your boxplots

# Example: Save soil temperature letters
# write.csv(landscape_letters_list[["Soil Temp"]], 
#           "letters_soil_temp_landscape.csv", 
#           row.names = FALSE)


```
